name: Coast Guard Sector Long Island Sound
tagline: The Coast Guard guarding Long Island Sound and the Connecticut coast, based at New Haven.
_note: Coast Guard Sector Long Island Sound (New Haven, CT). CONUS full template (BAH MHA CT051 New Haven/Fairfield; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). CG uses same BAH tables. SAR + law enforcement across Long Island Sound. Medical: CG clinic + TRICARE network. Facts verified from official sources (uscg.mil, TRICARE, CGX, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Connecticut is TRICARE East. Higher-cost SW Connecticut. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 06512 (East Haven).

data.Role: Coast Guard hub for Long Island Sound and the Connecticut coast
data.Mission: Search and rescue, port security, and law enforcement on the Sound
data.Setting: Southwestern Connecticut coast (higher cost of living)
data.Region: Between New York City and southern New England

source.label: Coast Guard Sector Long Island Sound (official)

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Coast Guard Sector Long Island Sound, based at New Haven, guards the busy waters of Long Island Sound and the Connecticut coast, running search and rescue, port security, and law enforcement.
It's in southwestern Connecticut between New York City and southern New England, a higher-cost coastal region with strong schools and lots of history.

source.label: Coast Guard Sector Long Island Sound (official)

sections.title: Connecticut Shoreline Living: Pricey but Strong Schools

data.subtitle: Southwestern Connecticut is desirable and expensive, with well-regarded schools. The main choice is which shoreline town fits your commute and budget.
intro: This part of Connecticut carries higher housing costs and property taxes, but schools are strong. Coast Guard housing is limited, so most families rent or buy.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across New Haven and the shoreline towns east and west, with Metro-North rail shaping options.

recommendations.advice: New Haven and East Haven keep the commute short.
Branford, Guilford, and Madison to the east are popular for schools and coastal charm.
Connecticut property taxes run high; factor them into renting vs buying.
Metro-North along the shoreline opens up options; factor transit into where you live.

source.label: Coast Guard Sector Long Island Sound (official)

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD/CG ID need to check in at the visitor control point.
Requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Coast Guard site before you go.

source.label: Coast Guard Sector Long Island Sound (official)

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Coast Guard family housing in the area is limited, so most families rent or buy across the New Haven area and the shoreline.
Eligibility and availability vary, so start with the Coast Guard housing office for current options and waitlists.

source.label: Coast Guard Sector Long Island Sound (official)
DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA CT051 (New Haven/Fairfield) — Coast Guard uses the same BAH tables

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. New Haven, East Haven, Branford, Guilford, and Madison districts serve military families depending on where you live; the shoreline towns are well regarded.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Clinic: Coast Guard clinic, Sector Long Island Sound
data.Care: On-base clinic care for the assigned population; most family care is via the TRICARE network and area hospitals (including Yale New Haven)

data.Commissary: Nearest DoD commissary is at Naval Submarine Base New London (Groton)

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and southwestern Connecticut's high prices and property taxes make renting common.
rent: Your tour is a standard length and you'll PCS out.
Connecticut prices and property taxes give you pause.
You want flexibility in an expensive market.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect a longer tour or repeat New England assignments.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
A shoreline town fits your budget for a home you like.
You're comfortable with Connecticut property taxes.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but high prices and taxes make renting common in this area. If you buy, weigh property taxes carefully. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Check housing
steps.text: Get the housing office's read on New Haven and the shoreline towns (and the property-tax picture) before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Connecticut vehicle registration, and learn the visitor process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (New Haven, Branford, Guilford, or Madison) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment and pick a network provider; the clinic serves the assigned population.

data.q: What is Sector Long Island Sound?
data.a: It's the Coast Guard's hub for Long Island Sound and the Connecticut coast, based at New Haven, handling search and rescue, port security, and law enforcement.

data.q: Do Coast Guard families get BAH?
data.a: Yes, the same BAH tables as the rest of the military. It's MHA CT051 (New Haven/Fairfield).
Across the New Haven area and the shoreline towns (Branford, Guilford, Madison), which have strong schools. CG housing is limited.
Yes. Southwestern Connecticut has higher housing costs and property taxes, so renting is common.
